headline: SOSMA renewal passed without parliamentary debate in 2024
context: SOSMA's 5-year renewal was passed by Parliament in December 2024 with minimal debate, extending the security law until 2029.

cards.big: SOSMA renewed until 2029 with only 45 minutes of parliamentary debate
cards.sub: Law enabling detention without trial extended with minimal scrutiny

cards.big: SOSMA Section 4(5) requires parliamentary renewal every 5 years
cards.sub: 2024 renewal motion allocated 45 minutes compared to 12 hours for original 2012 debate

cards.big: 147 SOSMA detainees in 2025 had no opportunity to challenge renewal process
cards.sub: Affected individuals have no standing in parliamentary renewal proceedings

cards.big: Only 23 of 222 MPs participated in the December 2024 renewal debate
cards.sub: Low participation suggests security laws face insufficient legislative scrutiny

cards.big: Civil society coalition submitted 15,000-signature petition opposing renewal
cards.sub: Petition was not acknowledged during the abbreviated parliamentary session

cards.big: 45 minutes to extend 5 years of preventive detention is not democratic oversight
Like renewing a building's safety exemption in 45 minutes without inspecting the building — the paperwork moves faster than the scrutiny
cards.sub: Five more years of preventive detention powers approved with less debate than a single reading of most bills

cards.big: A law that permits detention without trial deserves more than 45 minutes of scrutiny every five years. Renewal is not review.
